reasoning: The question asks whether State Street Corporation's (STT) stock price will be higher on March 22, 2026, than on March 15, 2026 (both fall on Sunday, so it translates to whether the price on Friday, March 20 will be higher than the price on Friday, March 13). Weekly short-term stock price movements closely follow a random walk with a very slight upward drift reflecting general market returns and inflation. While the tool's linear trend outputs a 0.54 probability of being higher and a combined seasonal forecast of 0.44, the combined estimate is based on absolute price thresholds from previous years, which is unreliable given long-term multi-year trends. In absence of any major known scheduled events (like earnings announcements or ex-dividend dates, the latter of which typically occurs around April 1) in the upcoming week, I anchor on the lack of predictability for a 1-week resolution horizon. Given the instruction to keep forecasts close to 50% for weekly movements unless a strong signal dictates otherwise, I estimate the probability slightly above 0.5 to account for the historical long-term positive skew in equities.
